commit.message:
Fix Discord bot: event-driven Ready instead of blocking entersState timeout

The 30s entersState(Ready) await was timing out on Railway's network, causing
process.exit(1) and a restart loop. Now we listen for the Ready event and
start streaming only when it fires — no blocking await, no process.exit on
voice timeout. Also fixed deprecated 'ready' -> 'clientReady' event name.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix .gitignore excluding src/etch_sim/output/ package from git

The unanchored `output/` rule matched directories at any depth,
which prevented the output subpackage from being tracked. This
caused ModuleNotFoundError in the Docker container. Anchor both
output/ and test_output/ to repo root with leading slash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add real computed data for Section 4 charts

Generated from 1M-game simulation (simulation_raw.bin) and category_sweep:
- category_landscape.json: 15 categories with variance decomposition
- category_pmfs.json: per-category score distributions from simulation
- mixture.json: 4 sub-populations (bonus x Yatzy), real fractions/means
- bonus_covariance.json: 50 + 10.3 + 11.7 = 72.0 decomposition
- graph_race_to_63.json: forward-pass upper-score evolution (838 nodes)
- graph_category_sankey.json: category timing (224 entries)
- graph_ev_funnel.json: EV spread by turn (1031 entries)

Also fixes category-landscape chart to handle negative variance contributions
(Ones anti-correlates with total score) and adds generation script.

commit.message:
Optimisations performance, sécurité et structure

Sécurité:
- Suppression logs session ID (AuthController)
- Rate limiting login réduit à 5/min
- Nouveau middleware SecurityHeaders (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options...)
- Nouveau middleware PublicPageCache (Cache-Control pages publiques)
- Validation téléphone renforcée + email DNS validation
- Erreurs MailSettings maintenant loguées

Performance:
- Fix N+1 queries BlogController (3 requêtes → 1)
- Cache menu 5min → 30min, settings DB mis en cache 30min
- Cache-busting CSS/JS via filemtime() (plus time())
- Scripts JS avec defer, preconnect CDN
- lazy loading image footer + dimensions déclarées
- Migration index DB: blog_posts, users, services

Structure:
- Widgets dupliqués Conseiller supprimés (6 fichiers)
- Schedule Artisan défini (Google Reviews 6h, Zoho nuit)
- composer.lock.bak supprimé

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add exponential backoff and retry limits to stream reconnect

Address PR review: reconnect now uses exponential backoff (3s → 6s →
12s → ... up to 60s) with a max of 15 attempts. All error paths in
initializePreview schedule retries instead of silently giving up.
After max attempts, shows "click to retry" message. Attempts reset
on successful connection or camera change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify InputUnpackedDim → §A.2.1.2 [skip ci]

The test parses `input logic d [3:0]`, which is an input port declaration with an unpacked dimension. §A.2.1.2 defines the `input_declaration` BNF rule (`input variable_port_type list_of_variable_identifiers`), where the trailing `[3:0]` is an unpacked dimension carried by `list_of_variable_identifiers`. This is the most specific subclause governing this construct.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(gsm): symmetric V-shape arrows + inline name editing

Arrow symmetry:
- Both OUT and IN curves share identical endpoints (ptA near
  subscriber circle edge, ptB near card edge with 8px gap)
- OUT (A→B) bows left, IN (B→A) bows right — perfect mirror
- Perpendicular offset from midpoint creates clean V-shape fan

Inline editing (replaces prompt() popups):
- Click on "✎ dodaj nazwę" or existing label → <input> overlay
  appears directly on the SVG text position
- Input styled with border, shadow, centered over the text
- Enter saves, Escape cancels, blur auto-saves
- Works for both contact cards and subscriber (center) node
- Prevents duplicate inputs; re-renders graph after save

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix oracle builder skipping backward induction when cached table exists

The oracle builder called compute_all_state_values_impl with skip_cache=false,
so when a cached strategy table existed on disk, it loaded the cached values
and returned early without running backward induction. This left the oracle
arrays zeroed, causing the forward pass to crash with no active states.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix login: use explicit select in auth queries to avoid missing column errors

The database schema is out of sync with the Prisma model — some columns
(like department, mustChangePassword) may not exist yet. Queries without
explicit select try to fetch ALL columns and fail with 'column not available'.
Using select ensures we only query columns that exist.

commit.message:
fix CS9007 by using plain raw string with .Replace() for prompt

Triple closing braces in nested JSON example cause CS9007 with
dollar-prefixed raw strings. Switch to a non-interpolated raw string
and inject variables via .Replace() to avoid all brace conflicts.

commit.message:
Feat: OS keychain integration for API key storage

API keys are no longer stored in plaintext in the config file.
Keys are stored in the OS keychain (macOS Security framework /
Linux kernel keyring via keyutils syscalls) and loaded into
memory at runtime only.

- New `alex-core::keychain::KeychainStore` abstraction with
  env-var fallback (ALEXANDRIA_API_KEY_<NAME>) for CI/headless
- `BackendEndpoint.api_key` marked `skip_serializing` — never
  written to TOML; `has_api_key` sentinel tracks existence
- One-time auto-migration of legacy plaintext keys on first load
- `AlexConfig::hydrate_api_keys()` populates keys from keychain
  before requests are made (CLI ping, orchestrator pipeline)
- Custom `Debug` impl for `BackendEndpoint` redacts key value
- Adversarial fixes: whitespace-only env var rejected, null-byte
  names rejected, orphaned keychain entries cleaned on re-add,
  migration sentinel corrected on keychain failure
- 72 tests in alex-core covering security invariants, migration,
  edge cases, and concurrency
